faq.q: What is the Red Tide fishing boat best for?
faq.a: The Red Tide is best for serious offshore sport fishing in Marbella. It's a dedicated fishing vessel designed for chasing tuna, dorado, and other pelagics in the Mediterranean.

faq.q: How many people can the Red Tide accommodate?
faq.a: The Red Tide can carry up to 6 passengers, making it perfect for small groups of anglers who want a focused, no-distractions fishing trip.

faq.q: Does the Red Tide depart from Puerto Banús?
faq.a: Yes, all our boats including the Red Tide depart from Puerto Banús, the main marina in Marbella. You'll meet the skipper at the dock.

faq.q: What is included in the Red Tide charter price?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%).

faq.q: How much does a Red Tide fishing charter cost?
faq.a: Pricing for the Red Tide is on request via WhatsApp. We tailor trips to your needs, so contact us for a custom quote based on duration and destination.

faq.q: What types of fish can I catch on a Red Tide trip?
faq.a: In Marbella's waters, you can target tuna, dorado, sea bass, and other sport fish. The skipper knows the best spots depending on season and conditions.

faq.q: Is the Red Tide suitable for beginners?
faq.a: Yes, beginners are welcome. The skipper provides guidance on fishing techniques, so you can enjoy the experience even if you've never fished before.

faq.q: Can I bring my own fishing gear on the Red Tide?
faq.a: Yes, you're welcome to bring your own rods and tackle. The boat also comes with basic fishing equipment, but serious anglers often prefer their own gear.

data.body_html:
The Red Tide fishing boat is the best choice in Marbella for serious offshore sport fishing. At 11 metres long and with a capacity of 6 passengers, this purpose-built vessel departs from Puerto Banús and takes you straight to the prime fishing grounds off the Costa del Sol. Whether you're chasing bluefin tuna or dorado, the Red Tide delivers a focused, no-frills angling experience that casual day cruisers can't match.

Why the Red Tide is the best boat for offshore sport fishing in Marbella

The Red Tide is not a luxury yacht — it's a fishing machine. With a beamy 11m hull and a single-engine layout optimised for trolling and drifting, it handles the open Mediterranean with stability. The deck layout gives you plenty of room to fight a fish, and the skipper knows the local waters intimately. From the deep drop-offs off Cabopino to the currents near Estepona, you'll be put on the fish fast.

Key specifications of the Red Tide fishing boat

Here's what you need to know about the Red Tide:

  • Length: 11 metres
  • Passenger capacity: 6 people
  • Departure point: Puerto Banús, Marbella
  • Boat type: dedicated sport fishing vessel
  • Inclusions: licensed skipper, fuel, drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, Spanish IVA (21%)
  • Pricing: on request via WhatsApp — contact us for a custom quote

What you can catch on a Red Tide charter

The Mediterranean off Marbella offers a variety of sport fish. Depending on the season, you can target bluefin tuna (often over 100 kg), dorado, sea bass, mackerel, and even occasional swordfish. The skipper adjusts the plan based on sea conditions and recent catch reports. For serious anglers, the Red Tide is the best boat for offshore sport fishing because it's built for the job — not for sunbathing.

How to book the Red Tide fishing boat

Booking is simple. Visit our Boat Rental Marbella homepage and select the Red Tide from the fleet. Because pricing is customised per trip (hours, fuel, destination), we ask you to get in touch via WhatsApp for a quote. Typical half-day (4-hour) trips start from around €1,200, but we'll confirm the exact price based on your plans.

Other fishing boats in our Marbella fleet

If the Red Tide isn't available, we also have the Red Tide fishing boat best for sport fishing, plus other vessels like the Bandido (11.5m, 8 pax) and the Speedboat (7m, 6 pax). For larger groups, consider the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax) which can also be used for fishing trips. Check our full boat rental Marbella fleet for more options.

What's included in your Red Tide charter

Every Red Tide charter includes a licensed skipper who handles navigation and fishing advice, fuel for the trip, a selection of drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava), light snacks, full insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no hidden extras — just turn up at Puerto Banús with your gear and enthusiasm.

Best times of year for offshore fishing in Marbella

The fishing season runs from April to October, with peak tuna action from June to September. Water temperatures range from 18°C in spring to 26°C in August. The skipper will advise on the best months for your target species. For serious offshore sport fishing, the Red Tide is the best boat in Marbella — book early to secure your date.

Frequently asked questions

What is the Red Tide fishing boat best for?

The Red Tide is best for serious offshore sport fishing in Marbella. It's a dedicated fishing vessel designed for chasing tuna, dorado, and other pelagics in the Mediterranean.

How many people can the Red Tide accommodate?

The Red Tide can carry up to 6 passengers, making it perfect for small groups of anglers who want a focused, no-distractions fishing trip.

Does the Red Tide depart from Puerto Banús?

Yes, all our boats including the Red Tide depart from Puerto Banús, the main marina in Marbella. You'll meet the skipper at the dock.

What is included in the Red Tide charter price?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%).

How much does a Red Tide fishing charter cost?

Pricing for the Red Tide is on request via WhatsApp. We tailor trips to your needs, so contact us for a custom quote based on duration and destination.

What types of fish can I catch on a Red Tide trip?

In Marbella's waters, you can target tuna, dorado, sea bass, and other sport fish. The skipper knows the best spots depending on season and conditions.

Is the Red Tide suitable for beginners?

Yes, beginners are welcome. The skipper provides guidance on fishing techniques, so you can enjoy the experience even if you've never fished before.

Can I bring my own fishing gear on the Red Tide?

Yes, you're welcome to bring your own rods and tackle. The boat also comes with basic fishing equipment, but serious anglers often prefer their own gear.
